page: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and memory bandwidth for small cached blocks. It also achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations and medium-model LLM text generation. Single-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations register in the strong category. However, single-threaded gzip compression and large-model LLM prompt processing perform at average levels.

Given its dedicated core allocation and DDR5 memory architecture, the instance offers high resource density and compute efficiency for multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options. This instance is suited for software compilation, database operations, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmarks, the m7a.16xlarge delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and small-block cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong performance in database operations and image processing. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software development pipelines, database management, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 256 GB of DDR5 memory.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC general-purpose instance with DDR5 memory, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores running at 3.7 GHz
256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 4 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and memory bandwidth performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference
